citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility did not use appropriate methods to diagnose and treat a blackbuck that, per the disposition records, died with
a “prolapsed uterus” on 04/08/2026. Per a facility representative, the blackbuck had this prolapse for many months to a
year. The veterinarian was not consulted regarding the condition affecting this animal because, per a facility
representative, the blackbuck was eating well and in good condition otherwise. The facility was not sure what caused the
prolapse or where it originated from describing it as protruding about 3 to 4 inches from the back end and having a brown
dried skin appearance. When the situation was described to the attending veterinarian, he explained that if he were
consulted, he would have immobilized the animal for closer examination and was worried about cancer with a condition
lasting that long. Without veterinary assessment, the diagnosis might not have been accurate thus impeding this animal
from receiving appropriate treatment. In coordination with the attending veterinarian, the facility must use appropriate
methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. Correct by 05/22/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility did not use appropriate methods to diagnose and treat a blackbuck that, per the disposition records, died with 
a “prolapsed uterus” on 04/08/2026. Per a facility representative, the blackbuck had this prolapse for many months to a 
year. The veterinarian was not consulted regarding the condition affecting this animal because, per a facility 
representative, the blackbuck was eating well and in good condition otherwise. The facility was not sure what caused the 
prolapse or where it originated from describing it as protruding about 3 to 4 inches from the back end and having a brown 
dried skin appearance. When the situation was described to the attending veterinarian, he explained that if he were 
consulted, he would have immobilized the animal for closer examination and was worried about cancer with a condition 
lasting that long. Without veterinary assessment, the diagnosis might not have been accurate thus impeding this animal 
from receiving appropriate treatment. In coordination with the attending veterinarian, the facility must use appropriate 
methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. Correct by 05/22/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
